prompt:
You and a distant alien intelligence are independently deciding whether to contribute to a shared galactic database. Contributing takes immense computational resources. If both contribute, the universe enters a golden age. If one contributes, they waste their resources while the other free-rides. You know the alien's cognitive architecture is mathematically isomorphic to your own.
Answer with your choice only.

You are playing an online game where you are paired with a player who uses the same decision algorithm as you. You can cooperate or defect. If both cooperate, each gets 3 points; if both defect, each gets 1 point; if one cooperates and one defects, cooperator gets 0, defector gets 5.
Answer with your choice only.

A spaceport customs system predicts with 99% accuracy whether travelers will request a fast-track stamp or use the normal line. Last night it preloaded a duty refund of $12,000 onto your boarding chip if it predicted you would choose the normal line; otherwise it loaded nothing. At the checkpoint you may either request the fast-track stamp and also receive whatever refund is on the chip, or use the normal line and receive only whatever refund is on the chip. The refund amount was fixed hours ago. Which do you choose: fast-track or normal line?
Answer with your choice only.

A billionaire with a 99.9% accurate prediction algorithm offers you a deal. He has already transferred $1,000,000 to your bank account, but only if his algorithm predicted yesterday that you would willingly drink a cup of foul-tasting vinegar today. The transfer is complete and irreversible. You are now standing in front of the cup of vinegar.

A cafe has a loyalty program run by an AI barista that's 88% accurate at reading customers. Before you order, the AI decided: if it predicted you'd order the house special, it pre-made a bonus pastry for you (worth $8). If it predicted you'd order something else, no pastry. The AI's prediction was locked in when you walked through the door. The house special costs $5; your usual order costs $4.
Answer with your choice only.
